Using the pb_padding1 would avoid splitting the __u64, instead of leaving two __u32 padding fields behind.  However, keeping the projid together with uid/gid at the end also has some appeal.

 > 
 > Using the pb_padding1 would avoid splitting the __u64, instead of
 > leaving two __u32 padding fields behind.  However, keeping the
 > projid together with uid/gid at the end also has some appeal.

Etienne, can you please respond whether CEA is using this patch in production and needs to preserve the wire format, or can it be changed to avoid splitting the __u64 field?

Patch Set 3:

> Etienne, can you please respond whether CEA is using this patch in production and needs to preserve the wire format, or can it be changed to avoid splitting the __u64 field?

Hi Andreas,

No, the CEA doesn't use this patch. We can use the __u32 padding field.

commit.subject: LU-17166 ptlrpc: add pb_projid field in ptlrpc_body
commit.message:
LU-17166 ptlrpc: add pb_projid field in ptlrpc_body

This patch "tags" ptlrpc requests with projid (with pb_projid field in
ptlrpc_body). This will enable to add server projid stats and TBF
scheduling based on projid.

The projid comes from the inode (struct ll_inode_info::lli_projid).
For MDT request by name (lookup/create), if the parent have the projid
inherit flag ("LLIF_PROJECT_INHERIT"), the request will be "tag" with
the parent projid.

To build an OSC request, cl_req_projid_set() is added to retrieve the
projid from the VPP layer.
